author: kevaundray
content: <@976542403269910539> trying to get more information re zilkworm on 2 GPUs -- for the average case is it around 12 seconds?

author: kevaundray
content: Also does it include MPT verification?

author: somnergy
content: Right now around 10 seconds (another 15% improvement yet to go live). I am trying to hunt the worst blocks and i think bad ones around 15-20s

author: kevaundray
content: Ah nice, this is for amsterdam and the code passes spec tests?

author: kevaundray
content: I'm also guessing that with sp1, the numbers are a lot higher?

author: somnergy
content: This doesn't have Amsterdam merged yet, but we can probably cook it up. There's a ton of 32-bit optimization paths, so Amsterdam on Airbender would take some extra time.

author: somnergy
content: SP1's proving time is higher, probably in the neighbourhood of 40-45 seconds on single GPU, so probably 20s on 2 5090s

author: kevaundray
content: Ah airbender has different security parameters (I always forget this)

author: somnergy
content: We'll have a version with 100-bit soon and a WIP 128-bit security equivalent versions from Airbender team, I am told

author: kevaundray
content: Ah I see, I think for fairness(closer to endgame) the 128-bit version is what we would compare against with a smaller proof size (currently its about 1MB)

author: kevaundray
content: ZisK I believe is the only one that actually has the correct security level and proof size right now, others have varying levels, but most hover around 100

author: somnergy
content: Zilkworm-ZisK would collapse ethproofs into a singularity

author: somnergy
content:
It will take some time, much of this has been long-term WIPs and we'll take up ZisK when we clear out our plates, including massive code reviews pending against the existing Airbender integration work.
It'll be easy to deliver Hypercube with Amsterdam, followed by Airbender with Amsterdam and then Zisk

author: kevaundray
content: I think the main thing is having the guest program working with amsterdam for native execution and passing tests, because the integration work can be done by multiple people

author: somnergy
content: Yeah, i think a vanilla version can be made available too (without optimizations or precompiles), it will work and pass tests. Amsterdam is underway, we'll have it soonish
